subject: Archive helm_charts with command module
commit_message:
Archive helm_charts with command module

Due to bug in ansible archive module empty files are not added into tar file.
Hence kibana-onborading.json missing in helm_charts on infra node.
Changing archive module into command module to tar helm_charts.

Issue-ID: OOM-2231
Signed-off-by: Ondřej Šmalec <o.smalec@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: I5527bd0f52976a93ac2861d9de010fbb79eb97fd

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

It's not idempotent right?
And hidden because that test is disabled: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=oom/offline-installer.git;a=blob;f=ansible/roles/application/molecule/default/molecule.yml;h=30c752e25fe472dd9074298d870d0dbda1cf8803;hb=HEAD#l43
Removed Code-Review-2 by Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

Patch Set 5:

Samuli is adding "changed_when: false" to this task to fake it's idempotence is ok with you?

For this particular issue (which this commit is addressing) we're not able to provide a clean solution that wouldn't be more or less hacky so there needs to be some kind of compromise in whatever way we deal with it.

Patch Set 5:

How idempotent was that archive module? Do we know? We could make this idempotent too, by first checking in the destination that these helm_charts are there (if not the continue), then create archive there and store checksum of it in some ansible variable and throw that archive away, then do the actual tar in the source, make checksum of it and compare two checksums, and act on the result.

So it is possible in more than one way to satisfy this idempotency, but do we care? Let's say: it is idempotent and on the destination is outdated version of helm_charts - does ansible archive module rewrite it with newer version? I think yes, so the only benefit of idempotency is that it tells us: "changed" and not just "ok"

am I right?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> How idempotent was that archive module? Do we know?

Yes, we do: archive module deals with it internally by creating working archive and comparing it's uniqueness property with resulting archive (if it already exists) and returns "not changed" in case they're the same

> We could make this idempotent too, by first checking (...)

In this review we're discussing creating archive idempotency so i find your remarks as utterly valid but out of scope as you're referencing the whole process idempotency, not this task alone. Please also note that unpacking process is done by "unarchive" module which is idempotent on it's own so i don't see an issue here.

> So it is possible in more than one way to satisfy this idempotency, but do we care? Let's say: it is idempotent and on the destination is outdated version of helm_charts - does ansible archive module rewrite it with newer version? I think yes, so the only benefit of idempotency is that it tells us: "changed" and not just "ok"
> 
> am I right?

My above comment also addresses this one of yours - whatever happens on dest is unarchive's module job and out of scope of this changeset i think.

Patch Set 7:

@bartek I was thinking that archive can also transfer to another machine and that is what we are doing, but if it just creates a tra next to a tarred directorz, then it is even simpler and my ide of using working/temp tar is the same in both, unarchive module should in the case of the idempotencz untar the archive onlz if there are changes - so that is why I dont see it as independent steps

Patch Set 7: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/686/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/686

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> @bartek I was thinking that archive can also transfer to another machine and that is what we are doing, but if it just creates a tra next to a tarred directorz, then it is even simpler and my ide of using working/temp tar is the same in both, unarchive module should in the case of the idempotencz untar the archive onlz if there are changes - so that is why I dont see it as independent steps

Unarchive module has the transferring capability and that's how we use it in "Unarchive helm charts on infra node" task.

Performing "unarchive" task based on changed status of "archive" task is not legitimate because unarchive has it's own idempotency logic and it has to be evaluated each time (i.e. it has to be always run because it decides upon it's own idempotency logic whether extract files on remote or not; please see idempotency remarks in https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/modules/unarchive_module.html)

Patch Set 7:

adding "changed when false" was not my suggestion. Even that can be used to fool molecule test. But the point was that molecule idempotence test is disabled for this whole module. And at some point we could fix it?
For user it would matter more that playbook just can be run again if it fails (not real idempotency meaning it only makes changes when it's not yet done). Is this now re-runnable?

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> adding "changed when false" was not my suggestion. Even that can be used to fool molecule test. But the point was that molecule idempotence test is disabled for this whole module. And at some point we could fix it?
> For user it would matter more that playbook just can be run again if it fails (not real idempotency meaning it only makes changes when it's not yet done). Is this now re-runnable?

While I agree with your remarks please note that this patch is in no way targeted at fixing idempotency in the scope of whole application.yml and wasn't supposed to, it's different topic. Idempotency matter here popped out only in the scope of the changed task code (helm charts archival)

Please raise a ticket for it if you'd like to see it fixed at some point.

Yes I'm completely aware of the topic of this commit, not so senile yet.
It's just our duty to comment these commits and this change decrease idempotence further more so why not to comment on that and not to decrease it.

This problem of application.yml is known from the beginning and we surely have a ticket from that. But wait...I just checked and did not found it????
So created it https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-2237

Patch Set 7:

I'm not sure how much we want to test idempotency there but I've tested running that tar command twice and it rewrites existing tar file with newer data. It is same sized tar file but newer. If this is only thing what is important (to be able to run this command twice and not to create another tar file or add content to existing tar file) then its OK.

I'm rather have kibana pod fixed (even as temp solution, because archive module is already fixed but still not in stable release of ansible) then exclude it from HC and whitelist it as we want to keep kibana in onap-me for now.

>

If this is only thing what is important (to be able to run this command twice and not to create another tar file or add content to existing tar file) then its OK.
> 
> I'm rather have kibana pod fixed (even as temp solution, because archive module is already fixed but still not in stable release of ansible) then exclude it from HC and whitelist it as we want to keep kibana in onap-me for now.

Great, for me it's enough that it's re-runnable like before this change. And as we now have a ticket to handle application play idempotense, we have done the purpose of review here.
Change has been successfully merged by Samuli Silvius
